portal_confirmation.subject: Police Records Request :: P002732-030626
portal_confirmation.body_excerpt:
Dear Documentary Researcher, Dr Insanity Media Sam Hylton:

Thank you for your interest in public records of City of Lubbock. Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with Chapter 552 of Texas Government Code, the Public Information Act. Your request was received in this office on 3/6/2026 and given the reference number P002732-030626 for tracking purposes.

Records Requested: Dear Records Custodian,
Pursuant to the public records act and/or the Freedom of Information Act, I am requesting
copies of the following records:
Body camera footage; 911/dispatch audio; Surveillance video; Interview/interrogation recordings
Subject: Ryan Campbell
Date of incident: Wed May 25 2022 00:00:00 GMT+0000 (Coordinated Universal Time)
I am willing to pay reasonable fees for the cost of copying these records. If the fees exceed
$25.00, please notify me before pr

portal_confirmation.subject: Longview Police Department-Record Request Complete PD-2026-366
portal_confirmation.body_excerpt: Dear Samuel Hylton, We have received your request for information under request PD-2026-366 dated 02/19/2026. We first notate that the case number "25163" is not a valid case number for the Longview Police Department. However, with the other information that you listed, it appears you are requesting Longview Police Report # 24-3676. This report and all related information, including video, audio, etc. involves at least one juvenile victim and is therefore entirely confidential under Section 261.201 of the Texas Family Code. Therefore, the city cannot release any portions of the records you requested. You or an attorney may be able to obtain the information with a subpoena or court order, if applicable. This request is now considered closed. Longview City Attorney's Office 903-237-1091

portal_confirmation.subject: Fort Collins Police Services - Criminal Just Records Request Confirmation PD-2026-1008
portal_confirmation.body_excerpt: Dear Requestor, Thank you for submitting a request for records on 02/26/2026. Your request reference number is Request Number: PD-2026-1008 and your security key is 6D65A91C. Please have both reference numbers available when communicating with our staff regarding your request. In accordance with Colorado Revised Statutes, 19-1-301 through 304, concerning Children’s Code Records and Information Act and 24-72-201 through 206, concerning Inspection, Copying and Photographing Public Records, and 24-72-301 through 24-72-309 concerning Criminal Justice Records, FCPS will provide, for public inspection, records in the custody of the agency which are legally allowed within the provision of the above referenced statutes. All requests are processed as soon as possible, but may take up to 7 working days. Such period may be extended if extenuating circumstances exist such as the request is for an active file; an unusually large request or the records need to be reviewed by administration. Your request may require approval through the District Attorney’s Office or City Attorney’s Office. Should your request be denied, you may request a written explanation as to why. Thank you for contacting For

portal_confirmation.subject: Records Request Confirmation
portal_confirmation.body_excerpt: Thank you for submitting a request for records. Your request security key is 7B8F608B. Your request reference number is Request Number: PRR-72-2026. Please have this security key and reference number available when communicating with our staff regarding your request.

portal_confirmation.subject: City of Bryan, TX - Records Request PD-2026-292 Cost Estimate
portal_confirmation.body_excerpt: Dear Samuel Hylton. : We have received your request for information dated: 02/18/2026. We have determined that complying with your request will result in the imposition of a charge that exceeds $40. Therefore, we are providing you with this cost estimate as required by section 552.2615 of the Texas Government Code. Cost for Labor and Body Worn Camera Videos 8 body worn camera videos at $10.00 per video $80.00 364 minutes of body worn camera videos at $1.00 per minute $364.00 TOTAL ESTIMATED RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS COSTS & LABOR COSTS: $444.00 There are some videos that are from the same time frame but different officers. If you would like to narrow the scope it may lower the total cost. There is a less expensive way for you to obtain this information. You may view the information in person at our offices. If you choose to view the information in person, please provide me with three dates and times when it will be convenient for you to come. Your request will be considered automatically withdrawn if you do not notify us in writing within ten business days from the date of this letter that you either: (a) accept the charges; (b) wish to modify your request; OR (c) have sent to the Open

Kristy [cid:image003.png@01DCA62D.6F23F980] Kristy Winslow | Senior Paralegal/ Supervisor Criminal Division Office of the Maine Attorney General 6 State House Station | Augusta, ME 04333 (207) 626-8505 (direct dial) | (207) 626-8800 (main office) Kristy.Winslow@maine.gov<mailto:Kristy.Winslow@maine.gov> | www.maine.gov/ag<http://www.maine.gov/ag> From: FOIA Request Team <sam@foib-request.com<mailto:sam@foib-request.com>> Sent: Thursday, February 19, 2026 10:44 AM To: Sean Lally <SLally@westbrook.me.us<mailto:SLally@westbrook.me.us>> Subject: Public Records Request - Marcel A. Lagrange Jr Hello Westbrook Police Department Records Team, I'm writing under Maine's Freedom of Access Act (1 M.R.S. �� 400-414) to request records related to the June 19, 2023 incident in downtown Westbrook, Maine involving Marcel A. Lagrange Jr. and the shooting of Brittney Cockrell and Michael Hayter (with two child witnesses) in/around a parked vehicle in a restaurant/shopping district area. If it's easiest, I'm happy to receive the full case file first. My main priority is video/audio. Please preserve any potentially

portal_access_issue.subject: Your Winnebago County - Sheriff, WI public records request #26-441 has been closed.
portal_access_issue.body_excerpt:
Winnebago County - Sheriff, WI ************************************************************************ Record request #26-441 has been closed. The closure reason supplied was: We closed this request after we were unable to contact you to assist you in making a focused and effective request that reasonably describes an identifiable record. If you believe that this office has withheld or delayed granting you access to a record in violation of law, you may bring an Action for Mandamus before the Circuit Court for Winnebago County and have the court order release of the records, or you may request the Winnebago County District Attorney’s Office or the Wisconsin Attorney General’s Office to do so, pursuant to §19.37, Wis Stats. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Winnebago County - Sheriff, WI.

Technical support: See our help page

portal_access_issue.subject: [External Message Added] Winnebago County - Sheriff, WI public records request #26-560
portal_access_issue.body_excerpt: Winnebago County - Sheriff, WI ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-560: Since you do not have any correlation to the videos, video redaction time is billed out. You would also need to sign a Certification and Understanding of Consequences for False Certification, which states in summary: I hereby certify as follows, with full knowledge of the legal consequences identified in Wis. Stat. § 19.35(3)(h)3a for false certification, including prosecution of me and a forfeiture of up to $10,000.00 for each violation by me. Wisconsin Statute § 19.35(3)(h)3a specifically provides the requester provides written certification to the authority that the requester will not use the audio or video content for financial gain. Any individual providing a false certification under this subd. 3. a. shall be subject to a forfeiture of $10,000 for each violation. The redaction time for the videos is billed out at the employee's hourly rate. An estimated cost would be billed out to you if you wish to proceed with your request. If you would like to proceed with your request, you will need to reply to this message and

portal_access_issue.subject: Your Winnebago County - Sheriff, WI public records request #26-560 has been submitted.
portal_access_issue.body_excerpt:
Winnebago County - Sheriff, WI ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-560 has been submitted successfully. Thank you for submitting your request to the Winnebago County Sheriff's Office. Please anticipate 3-10 business days for the processing and completion of your request. The length of time it takes to respond to the request depends upon several factors to include the nature and extent of the request as well as the availability of staff and other resources necessary to process the request. As the requester, you can always see the status of your request by signing into the Winnebago County - Sheriff, WI portal. If you haven't already signed in, you may need to activate or setup your account to get started. Once your account is activated, you can communicate directly with the Winnebago County - Sheriff, WI through NextRequest. ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact Winnebago County - Sheriff, WI.

Change your email settings |

Thank you for registering with the Lubbock, TX Open Records Center.
 
Your Login ID is:  sam@foib-request.com

If you have never used this system or cannot remember your password you may request a temporary password here:

mixed_partial_release.subject: Re: ** External Email Alert **  Public Records Request - Christian Goyne-Yarns
mixed_partial_release.body_excerpt: Good Afternoon, Your request has been received and is under review. Respectfully, Nicole Schwalbach, MA Open Records Custodian Strategic Communications Bureau Iowa Department of Public Safety 215 E. 7th St, Des Moines, IA 50319 Mobile: 515-559-7096 schwalba@dps.state.ia.us<mailto:schwalba@dps.state.ia.us> https://dps.iowa.gov/ https://dpscareers.com/ [cid:image003.jpg@01DA17E8.21881970] *NOTICE* This email message (including any file attachments transmitted with it) is for the sole use of the intended recipient(s) and may contain confidential and legally privileged information. Any unauthorized use or disclosure of this email by an unintended recipient is prohibited. If you have received this e-mail in error please notify the sender by return e-mail and destroy all copies of the original message. No representation is made that this email or any attachments are free of viruses. Virus scanning is recommended and is the responsibility of the recipient. ________________________________ From: FOIA Request Team <sam@foib-request.com> Sent: Wednesday, March 4, 2026 12:21 PM To: RecordsRequest <recordsrequest@dps.state.ia.us> Subject: ** External Email Alert ** Public Records Request - Chr

mixed_partial_release.subject: RE: Public Records Request
mixed_partial_release.body_excerpt: Dear Requester, RE: FOIA Request #2025-P-01234 We have completed our review of your public records request. Please be advised of the following determination: GRANTED IN PART / DENIED IN PART 1. Incident Report (RD# JE-456789): RELEASED with redactions. Personal identifying information of witnesses has been redacted pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(b) (privacy) and home addresses under 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(c). 2. Body-Worn Camera Footage (Officers Martinez, Badge #4521 and Thompson, Badge #8873): DENIED. This footage is being withheld in its entirety. The matter referenced in your request involves an ongoing criminal investigation (Case #2024-CR-88291). Release at this time would compromise the integrity of the pending prosecution per 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(d)(iv). Additionally, portions contain sensitive surveillance techniques exempt under 7(1)(d)(v). 3. 911 Call Recording: RELEASED without redaction. 4. Arrest Photos: DENIED under 5 ILCS 140/7(1)(d)(iv) - ongoing investigation. You may appeal this determination to the Public Access Counselor within 60 days. Records Management Division Chicago Police Department

Please also preserve any responsive video/audio that may still exist under your retention schedules while this request is pending. If it’s easier, you can provide the full case file first. wrong_agency_referral.subject: Fort Collins Police Services - Records Request
wrong_agency_referral.body_excerpt: Good afternoon Samuel Hylton, We have continued to receive record requests from you regarding an incident involving a Mr. Kenneth Piper. Fort Collins Police Services did not investigate or respond to this incident and therefore we have no responsive records. Mr. Piper was arrested by the Loveland Police Department, please contact their agency to request records involving this event. We have cancelled all six of your requests to our agency for these documents. All further requests for the same items will also be cancelled. We have sent correspondence through our request platform, JustFOIA: " We have reviewed your request. Regarding the incident you referred to in your request: Kenneth Piper, then 61, fatally shot his 38-year-old nephew, Justin Boothe, with a single gunshot to the chest inside Piper's residence at 1012 West Mulberry Street in Fort Collins, Larimer County, Colorado This incident did not occur in Fort Collins, but actually occurred in Loveland, Colorado (not at the Mulberry address) and was investigated by the Loveland Police Department. Please submit your records request to the Loveland Police Department. Fort Collins Police Services was not involved in this incident

If you believe that this office has withheld or delayed granting you access to a record in violation of law, you may bring an Action for Mandamus before the Circuit Court for Winnebago County and have the court order release of the records, or you may request the Winnebago County District Attorney’s Office or the Wisconsin Attorney General’s Office to do so, pursuant to §19.37, Wis Stats. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at Winnebago County - Sheriff, WI.
